ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "country" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the customer resides */,
  "gender" INTEGER /* Gender of the customer (0 for female, 1 for male) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the customer in years */,
  "annual_salary" REAL /* Annual salary of the customer */,
  "credit_card_debt" REAL /* Credit card debt of the customer */,
  "net_worth" REAL /* Net worth of the customer */,
  "car_purchase_amount" REAL /* Amount spent on car purchases by the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "number_of_dependents" INTEGER /* Number of dependents the customer has */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education the customer has completed */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Current occupation of the customer */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer (e.g., employed, self-employed, retired) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "tax_identification_number" TEXT /* Tax identification number of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_country FOREIGN KEY ("country") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used in the country */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Primary time zone of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "gdp" REAL /* Gross Domestic Product of the country */,
  "currency_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol used for the currency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_financials" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "annual_salary" REAL /* Annual salary of the customer */,
  "credit_card_debt" REAL /* Credit card debt of the customer */,
  "net_worth" REAL /* Net worth of the customer */,
  "car_purchase_amount" REAL /* Amount spent on car purchases by the customer */,
  "investment_value" REAL /* Value of the customer's investments */,
  "savings_account_balance" REAL /* Balance in the customer's savings account */,
  "loan_amount" REAL /* Amount of any current loans */,
  "monthly_expenses" REAL /* Monthly expenses of the customer */,
  "retirement_savings" REAL /* Retirement savings of the customer */,
  "health_insurance" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the customer has health insurance */,
  "life_insurance" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the customer has life insurance */,
  "investment_risk_tolerance" TEXT /* Risk tolerance level for investments (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_demographics" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the customer resides */,
  "gender" INTEGER /* Gender of the customer (0 for female, 1 for male) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the customer in years */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the customer */,
  "religion" TEXT /* Religion of the customer */,
  "household_income" REAL /* Total income of the customer's household */,
  "children" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the customer has children */,
  "spouse" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the customer has a spouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the transaction belongs to */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the transaction */,
  "transaction_amount" REAL /* Amount of the transaction */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., purchase, payment, transfer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transaction */,
  "transaction_category" TEXT /* Category of the transaction (e.g., groceries, utilities, entertainment) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the transaction occurred */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., credit card, debit card, cash) */,
  "transaction_status" TEXT /* Status of the transaction (e.g., completed, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_transactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "credit_scores" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "credit_score" INTEGER /* Credit score of the customer */,
  "score_date" TEXT /* Date when the credit score was last updated */,
  "credit_agency" TEXT /* Credit agency providing the score */,
  "credit_score_type" TEXT /* Type of credit score (e.g., FICO, VantageScore) */,
  "score_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the credit score (e.g., high credit utilization, late payments) */,
  "score_valid_until" TEXT /* Date until which the credit score is valid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_preferences" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "marketing_opt_in" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has opted in for marketing communications */,
  "communication_preference" TEXT /* Preferred method of communication (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communications */,
  "notification_preference" TEXT /* Preferred method of notifications (e.g., email, push notifications) */,
  "preferred_payment_method" TEXT /* Preferred method of payment */,
  "preferred_contact_time" TEXT /* Preferred time for contact (e.g., morning, evening) */,
  "marketing_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of marketing communications the customer prefers (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "privacy_settings" TEXT /* Privacy settings (e.g., share data with partners, do not share) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_relations" (
  "relation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "customer_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first customer in the relationship */,
  "customer_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second customer in the relationship */,
  "relation_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., spouse, parent, joint account) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the relationship started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the relationship ended (if applicable) */,
  "relationship_status" TEXT /* Status of the relationship (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  "relationship_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the relationship */,
  "relation_contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the related customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_relations_customer_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id_1") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_relations_customer_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id_2") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_liabilities" (
  "liability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each liability */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who has the liability */,
  "liability_type" TEXT /* Type of liability (e.g., mortgage, car loan, personal loan) */,
  "liability_amount" REAL /* Amount of the liability */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the liability */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the liability (e.g., active, paid, overdue) */,
  "liability_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the liability */,
  "liability_interest_rate" REAL /* Interest rate for the liability */,
  "liability_payment_history" TEXT /* Payment history for the liability */,
  "liability_guarantor" INTEGER /* ID of any guarantor for the liability */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("liability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_liabilities_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
